title: Prior anesthesia complication history

examination.text: Supports previous-anesthesia history, medical therapy, focused examination, airway and physical-condition assessment, and factors affecting perioperative risk.
examination.rationale: The ASA standard supports documenting previous anesthesia experiences and relevant medical therapy and physical condition without inferring that a prior event was an anesthesia complication.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports previous-anesthesia history, medical therapy, focused examination, airway and physical-condition assessment, and factors affecting perioperative risk.

follow_up.text: Supports review of records, interview about previous anesthesia experiences including prior awareness, focused risk assessment, and informing selected higher-risk patients without predicting awareness.
follow_up.rationale: The awareness-specific section was reviewed but not mapped because the broad history workflow does not establish awareness as the prior event.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of records, interview about previous anesthesia experiences including prior awareness, focused risk assessment, and informing selected higher-risk patients without predicting awareness.

source.title: Practice Advisory for Intraoperative Awareness and Brain Function Monitoring

source_limitations: Event type, date, procedure, technique, medicines, airway, hemodynamics, symptoms, severity, records, confirmed diagnosis, treatment, recovery, sequelae, family history, specialist advice, future plan, and patient communication remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
